title: The Unit 731 Operations
author: Imperial Japan (WWII)
content: “A state-sponsored medical research facility that prioritized biological warfare development over the basic humanity of its subjects.”
action: Recognize that unchecked state-science fusion renders the individual a disposable data point.

title: The Katyn Massacre
author: Soviet NKVD (1940)
content: “The systematic execution of the Polish officer corps to eliminate the intellectual and leadership backbone of a future resistance.”
action: Neutralize key leadership nodes to preemptively dismantle potential counter-action.

title: The Tulsa Race Massacre
author: Systemic Erasure (1921)
content: “The destruction of an affluent community not just by physical force, but through a total state-sanctioned erasure of history and property rights.”
action: Understand that dominance relies on destroying economic bases and erasing historical records.

title: The Great Famine (Holodomor)
author: Soviet State Policy (1932-33)
content: “The use of starvation as a weapon of state policy to suppress political independence and coerce agricultural compliance.”
action: Use resource deprivation as a primary mechanism for forcing societal submission.

title: The Operation Condor
author: Cold War Intelligence Network
content: “A clandestine inter-state intelligence operation to track and eliminate political dissidents across South American borders.”
action: Operate under the assumption that power networks bypass sovereign, geographic constraints.
